result:
**Queue (point-to-point):** one message → one consumer. Use when each message represents work that must be processed exactly once — job processing, order fulfillment, task distribution. Multiple consumers compete; the broker load-balances.

**Topic (pub/sub):** one message → N subscribers, each gets a copy. Use when the same event must fan out to independent consumers — e.g., "OrderPlaced" triggers billing, inventory, email, analytics separately.

Rule of thumb:
- "Who does this work?" → queue
- "Who needs to know?" → topic

Hybrids exist (Kafka consumer groups, SNS→SQS, JMS durable subscriptions) when you want pub/sub fan-out *and* load-balanced processing within each subscriber.
